So that's a greenfield.
Brownfield just simply means that they're an existing plant, they've got existing systems.
What happens oftentimes, especially in manufacturing,
is you might have a giant plant and part of it does this one process over here, and they might literally go sell off that process to another plant.

And so they, you know, another company comes in and they just have a piece of a plant, but that plant is totally brownfield because it doesn't integrate with their existing systems or anything that they already have.
They're just coming in and essentially buying a process.
So brownfield is really just existing or kind of legacy stuff that you might bring in.
